name: space_law_model_based_triage_tool_begins_millhaven_st_lt
premise: A Space Law response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving space law model based triage tool begins millhaven st lt. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is before the next automated batch runs. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as short term vs long term under pressure. A live dashboard is public; inconsistencies will trigger immediate investigation. Constraints: Preserve evidence under audit and litigation-hold. Make the intervention reversible and time-boxed with named accountability.

agents.name: Zara Petrov
agents.identity: I am Zara Petrov, accountable for a defensible call in Space Law when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays long term for short term.

agents.name: Jonah Petrov
agents.identity: I am Jonah Petrov, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Space Law.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so long term isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of short term.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect long term.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to long term to secure short term under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is before the next automated batch runs. Evidence is partial. Zara Petrov must decide under short term vs long term. What does Zara Petrov do?
